resources.name: Road Home Program
resources.description: The National Center of Excellence for Veterans and Their Families at Rush University Medical Center. Provides comprehensive mental health care including the Accelerated Treatment Program (ATP) for PTSD, TBI, and MST; intensive outpatient services; couples counseling; child counseling; caregiver support groups; survivor counseling; and the 'Do You Love a Vet' support group (1st Tuesday monthly, 6-7:30 PM). All services at NO COST regardless of discharge status. Serves veterans of ALL eras, service members, and families. Family defined as: spouses, partners, children, parents, siblings, caregivers. Illinois & Indiana: full outpatient services. PSYPACT states (43 states): Virtual Accelerated Treatment. Nationwide: Do You Love a Vet program and annual in-person ATP.
resources.contact_extra: 1645 W. Jackson Blvd., Suite 602, Chicago, IL 60612
resources.eligibility: Veterans of all eras, service members, and their families regardless of discharge status
resources.cost: Free - no cost for any services

resources.name: Veterans Crisis Line
resources.description: 24/7 crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families. If you or someone you know is in crisis, reach out immediately. Trained counselors are available around the clock.
resources.eligibility: All veterans, service members, and their families

resources.name: Military OneSource
resources.description: Free, confidential counseling and support services for active-duty, National Guard, Reserve service members, and their families worldwide, 24/7. Offers 12 free confidential counseling sessions per issue; face-to-face, phone, or online video counseling; specialty consultations (financial, legal, education); spouse education and career support; Military and Family Life Counselors (MFLCs) at installations.
resources.eligibility: Active-duty, National Guard, Reserve service members and families

resources.name: Emory Healthcare Veterans Program
resources.description: Located at Emory University in Atlanta, Georgia. Part of the Warrior Care Network. Provides two-week intensive outpatient treatment program, individual and group therapy, wellness workshops and mindfulness training, family participation opportunities. All treatment, travel, meals, and related expenses covered 100%.

resources.name: UCLA Health Veterans Program
resources.description: Located at UCLA in Los Angeles, California. Part of the Warrior Care Network. Offers accelerated brain health treatment, PTSD/TBI/MST specialized care, cohort-based treatment model (healing alongside fellow veterans), art therapy and collaborative activities. No-cost program covering all expenses.

resources.name: Massachusetts General Hospital Home Base
resources.description: Located at MGH in Boston, Massachusetts. Part of the Warrior Care Network. Delivers comprehensive mental health assessment and treatment, two-week intensive treatment program, evidence-based therapies by veteran mental health specialists, peer-to-peer support and education. All costs covered. Also serves pre-9/11 veterans and their families.
resources.eligibility: All era veterans and service members

resources.name: Child and Youth Behavioral Military and Family Life Counselors (CYB-MFLC)
resources.description: Specialized counselors providing free, confidential, non-medical counseling for military children and youth on installations, schools, and child development centers. Topics: deployment stress, anger management, bullying, self-esteem, sibling relationships, family dynamics.

resources.name: Our Military Kids
resources.description: Supports children (ages 5-12th grade) of deployed National Guard and Reserve service members and children of wounded warriors with grants for sports, fine arts, tutoring, and camps.
resources.eligibility: Children ages 5-12th grade of deployed Guard/Reserve or wounded warriors

resources.name: Military Child Education Coalition (MCEC)
resources.description: Provides resources and advocacy to ensure quality educational opportunities for all military children affected by mobility, family separation, and transition.

resources.name: VA Caregiver Support Program
resources.description: Offers training, educational resources, respite care, mental health services, and financial support for caregivers of veterans. Key programs: Program of Comprehensive Assistance for Family Caregivers (PCAFC), Program of General Caregiver Support Services (PGCSS), caregiver support groups and peer mentoring.
resources.eligibility: Caregivers of veterans and service members

resources.name: Elizabeth Dole Foundation - Hidden Heroes
resources.description: Connects military caregivers with national and local resources, support programs, and a community of fellow caregivers. Part of the Hidden Heroes campaign.

resources.name: Illinois National Guard Family Programs
resources.description: Comprehensive support for Illinois National Guard service members and families including family readiness groups, Family Assistance Centers (24/7 Hotline), Child and Youth Services, Employment Assistance Program, financial counseling and emergency assistance.
resources.eligibility: Illinois National Guard members and families

resources.name: Illinois Military Family Relief Fund (IMFRF)
resources.description: Provides monetary grants to families of Illinois National Guard members and Illinois residents serving in U.S. Armed Forces Reserve components called to active duty. Since 2003, has provided over $15.4 million to 28,840 families.
resources.eligibility: Illinois Guard/Reserve families called to active duty

resources.name: Illinois Joining Forces
resources.description: A network of over 200 community organizations serving service members, veterans, and families throughout Illinois.

resources.name: Illinois Warrior Assistance Program (IWAP)
resources.description: A warm helpline providing high-touch holistic resource navigation and case management for Illinois veterans, families, and caregivers experiencing emotional challenges. Specializes in PTSD, mild TBI, military sexual trauma, anxiety, depression, stress, addiction and substance abuse.

resources.name: Army Emergency Relief (AER)
resources.description: Provides financial assistance up to $2,500 with no repayment required for active-duty Army service members and families experiencing financial hardship due to deployment, military pay errors, or pending medical discharge.
resources.eligibility: Active-duty Army service members and families

resources.name: Navy-Marine Corps Relief Society
resources.description: Provides relief and recurring family support programs including critical financial assistance, housing support, holiday programs, and education loans.
resources.eligibility: Navy and Marine Corps members and families

resources.name: Air Force Aid Society
resources.description: Offers emergency assistance, educational support, and community programs for Air Force and Space Force families.
resources.eligibility: Air Force and Space Force families

resources.name: Coast Guard Mutual Assistance
resources.description: Provides financial assistance and support services to Coast Guard families during times of need.

resources.name: National Military Family Association (NMFA) Scholarships
resources.description: Scholarships for military spouses covering all degrees from GEDs to PhDs, professional licenses and certifications, business expenses, and continuing education. Open to spouses of all 8 Uniformed Services.
resources.eligibility: Military spouses of all 8 Uniformed Services

resources.name: Survivors' and Dependents' Educational Assistance (DEA - Chapter 35)
resources.description: Educational benefits for children or spouses of veterans who have died, are captured or missing, or have permanent and total service-connected disabilities.
resources.eligibility: Dependents of deceased, captured, or permanently disabled veterans

resources.name: Tutor.com - Military Families
resources.description: Free online tutoring and homework help available 24/7 for military families, including parent coaching.

resources.name: I AM WOC (International Association of Military Women of Color)
resources.description: Provides leadership development, mentoring, educational scholarships, and empowerment resources for military women of color, veterans, and their families. Hosts annual empowerment conferences.
resources.eligibility: Military women of color, veterans, and families

resources.name: American Veterans for Equal Rights (AVER)
resources.description: The oldest LGBTQ+ veterans organization in the US, founded in 1990. Provides advocacy, peer support, community connection for LGBTQ+ veterans. A member of the Road Home Program Outreach Team serves as President of the AVER Chicago Chapter.
resources.eligibility: LGBTQ+ veterans and active-duty service members

resources.name: Modern Military Association of America (MMAA)
resources.description: Serves LGBTQ+ service members, military spouses, veterans, and families. Policy advocacy, mental health support, crisis intervention, career transition assistance, family support programs, legal assistance.

resources.name: SPARTA Pride
resources.description: Dedicated to supporting transgender service members and families. Provides education, advocacy, peer mentorship, family support resources, and healthcare navigation assistance.

resources.name: PFLAG Connects: Military Community
resources.description: Support for LGBTQ+ service members, veterans, and military family members. Parent and family support groups, crisis support, counseling referrals, connection to local PFLAG chapters.

resources.name: NAMI Homefront
resources.description: Free six-session educational program for families, caregivers, and friends of military service members and veterans with mental health conditions.
resources.eligibility: Families and caregivers of veterans with mental health conditions

resources.name: Wounded Warrior Project (WWP)
resources.description: Mental health services for post-9/11 warriors and families at no cost, including interactive programs, rehabilitative retreats, and professional services. Also operates the Warrior Care Network.

resources.name: Give an Hour
resources.description: Connects service members, veterans, and families with free mental health care through a network of licensed providers who volunteer their time.

resources.name: Cohen Veterans Network
resources.description: Provides accessible, high-quality mental health care to post-9/11 veterans and military families through a national network of clinics.

resources.name: Vet Centers
resources.description: Community-based counseling centers providing readjustment counseling for veterans and families. Offers individual and group counseling, family counseling, substance abuse assessment, and employment assistance.

resources.name: Headstrong
resources.description: No-cost mental health care for post-9/11 veterans through a network of licensed civilian therapists. No insurance, referrals, or VA involvement needed.

resources.name: SAMHSA National Helpline
resources.description: 24/7 free, confidential treatment referral and information service for substance use disorders and mental health. Available in English and Spanish.
resources.eligibility: Anyone seeking substance use or mental health help

resources.name: Tragedy Assistance Program for Survivors (TAPS)
resources.description: Support for families grieving military deaths. Provides peer-based emotional support, grief counseling referrals, connections to community resources, and casework assistance.

resources.name: Team RWB
resources.description: Physical and social activities connecting veterans to their communities through fitness events, social gatherings, and service projects.

resources.name: Rosalynn Carter Mental Health and Caregiver Program
resources.description: Provides support, education, and resources specifically designed for military and veteran caregivers.

resources.name: Hidden Helpers Coalition
resources.description: Support for children and youth who serve as caregivers for wounded, ill, or injured service members and veterans in their families.

resources.name: Operation Purple Camp
resources.description: Free summer camps for military children offering outdoor activities, leadership development, and connections with peers who understand military life.

resources.name: FOCUS (Families OverComing Under Stress)
resources.description: Evidence-based resilience training for military families. Helps families develop skills to manage stress, communicate effectively, and build stronger connections.

resources.name: Military Kids Connect
resources.description: Online community for military children ages 6-17 providing age-appropriate resources to support children dealing with the unique psychological challenges of military life.

resources.name: Illinois Department of Veterans Affairs
resources.description: State benefits, programs, and resources for Illinois veterans including healthcare, housing assistance, employment, and education benefits.

resources.name: TRICARE
resources.description: Military health insurance program providing comprehensive health coverage for active-duty service members, retirees, and their families.

resources.name: Hiring Our Heroes
resources.description: U.S. Chamber of Commerce initiative providing career fairs, fellowship programs, employment resources, and hiring events for veterans, transitioning service members, and military spouses.
resources.eligibility: Veterans, transitioning service members, military spouses

resources.name: Blue Star Families
resources.description: Community programs, career development, and family support for military families. Conducts annual Military Family Lifestyle Survey providing critical data on military family challenges.

resources.name: Yellow Ribbon Reintegration Program
resources.description: Pre- and post-deployment support for National Guard and Reserve members and families. Provides information, services, and referrals throughout the deployment cycle.
resources.eligibility: National Guard and Reserve members and families

resources.name: Exceptional Family Member Program (EFMP)
resources.description: Support for military families with special needs members including medical, educational, and community support coordination.
resources.eligibility: Military families with special needs members

resources.name: Military & Family Life Counseling (MFLC)
resources.description: Short-term, non-medical counseling available on military installations. Provides confidential support without record-keeping. Does not impact security clearance.

resources.name: VA Substance Use Treatment
resources.description: Comprehensive substance use disorder treatment for veterans including counseling, medication-assisted treatment, and residential programs.

resources.name: National Suicide Prevention Lifeline
resources.description: 24/7 free and confidential support for people in distress. Available for anyone, not just veterans.

facing_crisis.description: If you or someone you know is in crisis right now

struggling_financially.description: Financial hardship or emergency assistance needed

resources: Rosalynn Carter Mental Health and Caregiver Program
caregiver_burnout.description: Caregiver feeling overwhelmed or burned out

key_statistics.source: 2025 Blue Star Families Military Family Lifestyle Survey
stats: 40% of active-duty families face waitlists for mental health care
44% experience waitlists for child mental health care
90% on child mental health waitlists waited 3+ months
28% of military families experience food insecurity
Only 44% report satisfaction with military life
68% say two incomes vital to financial stability
